differences.dallas: Your existing Texas Sales Tax Permit covers in-store sales.
differences.austin: Online sales to Texas customers require the same Texas Sales Tax Permit — already covered. However, you must now collect and remit sales tax on all Texas-destination orders, including those shipped from out-of-state inventory. Update your filing to include remote sales.

If you sell to customers in other states via your online store, you may trigger economic nexus in those states (typically $100,000 in sales or 200 transactions per year). Each state has separate registration and remittance requirements.

If using company vehicles for delivery, Texas requires commercial auto insurance. Vehicles over 26,000 lbs require a CDL and DOT number. Light delivery vehicles (vans, pickup trucks) need only standard commercial auto insurance.

differences.dallas: Your existing entity covers in-store retail.
differences.austin: If operating your e-commerce store under a different brand name than your registered entity, you must file a DBA (Assumed Name Certificate) with the Texas Secretary of State or your county clerk.
Adding e-commerce delivery does not typically require an additional city license unless you open a warehouse or fulfillment location. Verify with your city if you add a second physical site.
